full_references: Mahadevan, Iravatham. 1977. The Indus Script: Texts, Concordance and Tables. Memoirs of the Archaeological Survey of India, No. 77. New Delhi: ASI.
Parpola, Asko. 1994a. Deciphering the Indus Script. Cambridge: CUP. ISBN 978-0-521-43079-1.
Parpola, Asko. 1994b. Sign list (= Parpola 1994a fig. 5.1, pp. 70-78).
Wells, Bryan K. 2015. The Archaeology and Epigraphy of Indus Writing. Oxford: Archaeopress. ISBN 978-1-78491-046-4.
Parpola, Asko. 2010. A Dravidian solution to the Indus script problem. Coimbatore.

_doc: Mahadevan 1977 (M77) sign-list <-> Parpola 1994b (P) sign-list crosswalk for the most-cited and most-relevant signs. Source: Mahadevan 1977 Appendix III (sign list comparisons); Parpola 1994a fig. 5.1 + Parpola 1994b. Wells 2015 also cross-references in his sign list. NOTE: this is a curated subset; complete crosswalks for all ~400 signs require Mahadevan 1977 in print form.
method_note: Each entry maps M77 ID -> Parpola ID and includes any additional cross-references (Wells, ICIT (Fuls; accessed via Yajnadevam lipi) where known). Entries marked 'allograph_of' indicate this M77 sign is an allograph of another sign in Parpola's standard family.
version: Phase-28 v1, hand-curated from Parpola 2010 + Mahadevan 1977 secondary references

M126.iconic: rare in Indus, common in Near East

M016.iconic: Janabiyah seal pos 5; rare in Indus, common in Near East

fish._doc: Per Parpola 1994a Ch. 10 + Parpola 2010, all signs depicting a fish (plain, with roof, with fins, with trefoil, with horizontal lines, etc.) are interpreted as the same Dravidian rebus *miin* (fish/star). Signs 50, 52, 145, 147 add diacritical modifiers (likely vowel/consonant qualifiers in the underlying compound name).

numerals._doc: Parpola 1994a fig. 5.1 sequence 86-95 are the numerical signs. Sign 86 = 1, 87 = 2, ... 95 = 10. Compounds with fish (47) yield aru-miin (Pleiades, 6+fish), eZu-miin (Ursa Major, 7+fish), vaTa-miin (north star, fig+fish).

intersecting_circles.iconic: two intersecting circles (with various stroke variants)
intersecting_circles._doc: Parpola 2010 reads as muruku ('young man / Murukan / ear-ring / bangle'). The variant signs 262, 263 are likely allographs with minor stroke differences.

fig_tree._doc: Parpola 2010: the 'fig + fish' compound = vaTa-miin = north star. Sign 311 in Parpola 1994b numbering.
